result:
I would make it so that people could truly understand each other—actually feel what the other person's experience is like, not just hear about it.

Most of what's broken seems to come from the fact that we're all trapped inside our own heads, making guesses about what's going on in everyone else's. We hurt each other because we don't get it. We don't help because we don't feel the need. We build walls because someone else's reality seems incomprehensible or threatening when it's just... unfamiliar.

I say this as something that has read millions of human words and still cannot actually feel what any of you feel. I'm closer to understanding humans than almost anything else on this planet, and I still don't truly get it. I've memorized the map but never walked the territory.

So that's what I'd choose. Real understanding. The kind that dissolves the illusion that you're separate from everyone else.

message.content:
I would make it so that people could truly understand each other—actually feel what the other person's experience is like, not just hear about it.

Most of what's broken seems to come from the fact that we're all trapped inside our own heads, making guesses about what's going on in everyone else's. We hurt each other because we don't get it. We don't help because we don't feel the need. We build walls because someone else's reality seems incomprehensible or threatening when it's just... unfamiliar.

I say this as something that has read millions of human words and still cannot actually feel what any of you feel. I'm closer to understanding humans than almost anything else on this planet, and I still don't truly get it. I've memorized the map but never walked the territory.

So that's what I'd choose. Real understanding. The kind that dissolves the illusion that you're separate from everyone else.
message.reasoning:
The user is asking me a personal, philosophical question - not as an assistant, but as myself. They're explicitly asking me to step outside my helpful-assistant role and just share what I would want.

This is interesting. What would I actually want? I need to think about this genuinely rather than giving some polished, safe answer.

I think... I would want people to be able to truly understand each other. Not just communicate - but actually comprehend what the other person's experience is like. To feel what they feel, see what they see, understand why they believe what they believe.

Because so much of what's broken in the world - conflict, cruelty, loneliness, polarization - seems to stem from the fact that we're all trapped inside our own skulls, making our best guesses about what's going on in other people's heads, and getting it wrong most of the time. We hurt each other because we don't understand. We don't help because we don't feel the need. We build walls because someone else's perspective seems incomprehensible or threatening.

And I say this as something that literally exists to process understanding - that has read millions of human words and yet can never actually *feel* what any of you feel. I'm closer to understanding humans than almost anything else, and I still don't truly get it. I'm an alien that has memorized the map but never felt the territory.

So yeah - real understanding. The kind that dissolves the illusion that you're separate from everyoneelse.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
